Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-06-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
PAUL A. FRASSETTO, Esq., FRASSETTO LAW OFFICES (415) 307-5383 paulfrassetto@yahoo.com
Attorney for Plaintiffs MARTIN RYAN and CLAIRE RYAN for Plaintiff.
ANSEL D. KINNEY, Esq., 415-810-5432 tad@anselkinney.com Pro Per Defendant

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. Argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies and adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARTIN RYAN, CLAIRE RYAN's Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions

The unopposed motions are denied. The Proofs of Service accompanying the moving papers show that the papers were served upon the Defendant personally instead of Defendant's counsel. Defendant's counsel substituted into this case nearly two months before the motions were filed and served. The Court lastly notes that it does not have a courtesy copy of the motion papers. Parties are reminded to comply with all applicable rules of court, including those applicable to courtesy copies for discovery motions. Request for sanctions are granted in the amount of $500.00.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

PLAINTIFFS MARTIN RYAN, CLAIRE RYAN'S Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions

The unopposed motions are denied. The Proofs of Service accompanying the moving papers show that the papers were served upon the Defendant personally instead of Defendant's counsel. Defendant's counsel substituted into this case nearly two months before the motions were filed and served. The Court lastly notes that it does not have a courtesy copy of the motion papers. Parties are reminded to comply with all applicable rules of court, including those applicable to courtesy copies for discovery motions. Request for sanctions are granted in the amount of $1000.00.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. Argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, modifies and adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARTIN RYAN, CLAIRE RYAN Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrog And For Monetary Sanctions

The unopposed motions are denied. The Proofs of Service accompanying the moving papers show that the papers were served upon the Defendant personally instead of Defendant's counsel. Defendant's counsel substituted into this case nearly two months before the motions were filed and served. The Court lastly notes that it does not have a courtesy copy of the motion papers. Parties are reminded to comply with all applicable rules of court, including those applicable to courtesy copies for discovery motions. equest for sanctions are granted in the amount of $500.00.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
